Fresh prints overlap the path, and a hungry pack tests the party from the ferns before closing in.
Pale mushrooms grow in a perfect circle beneath a lightning-split oak, faintly cool to the touch.
A waxed note is tucked inside a birch hollow, asking whoever finds it to carry a warning deeper along the old game trail.
A storm-dropped cedar lies clear of rot, but its weight and tangled crown make clean cutting slow work.
Antlers, feathers, and twined grass hang from a cedar branch, marking a quiet boundary respected by local beasts.
You recognize the sign as a warning to skirt the old denning ground ahead.
The meaning is unclear until the forest falls suddenly, watchfully silent.

The meaning is unclear until the forest falls suddenly, watchfully silent.
A territorial boar bursts from thick brambles and charges anything blocking its route through the undergrowth.
A small hunting band shadows the trail, hoping to drive intruders into a hasty fight.
Kobolds concealed among fallen logs spring a simple trap and attack from cover.
A hungry bear emerges from a hollow beneath tangled roots and defends its feeding ground.
Strange lights lure travelers toward the hunting range of a patient forest beast.
An ill-tempered ogre claims a section of woodland as its own and drives off all visitors violently.
Medicinal herbs grow where a cold stream bends beneath alder roots.
Dark berries cling to thorny vines along a sunny clearing edge.
A small stack of stones marks a rarely used path crossing the woods.
You determine which trail remains safe and passable.
You lose time following misleading tracks.
A weathered pouch hidden beneath a loose marker stone escaped notice for years.
Fresh water bubbling from a mossy bank restores vigor after a short rest.
An old carved marker has toppled into a stream and nearby trails have become confusing.
A line of snares has gone silent and signs suggest something large is disturbing the area.
Smoke was seen near a crumbling woodland watchpost that should have been abandoned.
Strange lights wander near an overgrown ruin and several travelers have gone astray.
A stand of shrubs bears ripe nuts untouched by wildlife.
Brown-capped mushrooms hide among damp leaves beside a fallen oak.
Sharp-scented bulbs grow in loose soil near a sunny break in the canopy.
Fresh tracks crisscross a muddy trail junction.
You identify productive foraging grounds nearby.
You follow tracks that lead nowhere useful.
A tall pine offers long, usable lengths of timber if felled carefully.
A recently fallen oak remains solid and largely free of rot.
Lightning cracked a mature tree, leaving workable wood scattered nearby.
A damaged tree threatens to fall unpredictably during cutting.
You judge the fall correctly and work safely.
The tree shifts unexpectedly, slowing the effort.
Several mature hardwood trees grow close together on firm ground.
A dead limb breaks loose during cutting and crashes through the work area.
Silken strands drape between trees where a hungry spider waits for movement below.
Hidden archers use old hunting blinds and brush cover to spring a sudden attack along a narrow trail.
A weathered stone marker peeks from moss beside an overgrown fork in the trail.
You identify the older route and avoid losing time.
You follow a winding path that circles back on itself.
A cool breeze carries the scent of approaching rain through the pines, and birds grow quiet.
Dense brambles conceal hooked thorns that tear at clothing and exposed skin.
A stream crossing has fallen apart, forcing travelers onto a dangerous detour unless the route is restored.
Fresh claw marks scar several trees near a game trail, and local wildlife avoids the area.
An abandoned camp shows signs of a hurried departure, with tracks leading deeper into the woods.
Amber resin oozes from a wounded pine and can be collected with patience.
A productive oak grove has dropped a thick carpet of sound acorns.
A sheltered grove contains many fallen limbs suitable for fuel and simple camp work.
Several mature trees can spare strips of thick bark without lasting harm if taken carefully.

A deer carcass lies wedged in a shallow ravine, drawing competing scavengers that grow increasingly aggressive as anyone approaches. The narrow banks and tangled roots leave little room to maneuver once the creatures decide the intruders are rivals rather than passersby.

Fresh axe marks and stripped saplings suggest recent activity ahead. A small band of raiders waits where dense brush pinches the trail, hoping confusion and poor sightlines will break resistance before a proper defense can form.

Recent rain has swollen a normally harmless stream into a fast, debris-choked obstacle. Broken branches indicate others attempted the crossing recently, but uncertain footing and hidden channels make choosing a route a meaningful risk.
You identify a stable crossing and preserve time and supplies.
A poor route forces delays and leaves signs of your passage behind.

Unearthed by erosion, rusted fragments of weapons and scattered bones emerge from a low hillside. Animal tracks avoid the area, and the ground hints at an old conflict whose traces still shape movement through the surrounding woods.

A deadfall has exposed a carefully concealed cache beneath a root mass. Whoever hid it expected to return, but weather and years have reclaimed the site, leaving the contents vulnerable to the next traveler willing to investigate.

Thin smoke rises from a section of forest where no camp should stand. Reaching it requires navigating overgrown game trails and determining whether the source is a stranded traveler, opportunistic squatters, or something more dangerous.
A well-used route has become impassable beneath an unnatural surge of thorn growth. Travelers are being forced onto longer paths, and someone must determine the cause and reopen safe passage.

A sequence of old trail markers has been damaged or removed, leaving several woodland routes difficult to follow. Restoring the line means locating missing stones and learning what disturbed them in the first place.

A damp hollow shelters valuable herbs, but recent rooting by animals has churned the ground into slick mud. Careful harvesting is needed before the plants are trampled or washed away by the next storm.
A stand of mature beeches has produced an unusually heavy crop of edible nuts. Signs of feeding wildlife are everywhere, creating pressure to gather quickly before the opportunity disappears.

A towering fir broke high above the ground during recent winds and now hangs tangled among neighboring branches. The timber is valuable, but bringing it down safely requires judgment and controlled effort.
Several mature oaks grow on firm ground beside an abandoned trail. The wood is dense and desirable, yet careful selection is required to avoid wasting effort on trunks hiding internal rot.
